content.problemStatement:
Chef got another sequence as a birthday present. He does not like this sequence very much because it is not sorted. Since you are a good programmer, Chef is asking for your help.

You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and an integer KKK. You may perform any number of operations of the following type (in any order): choose a valid integer iii and swap AiA_iAi​ with Ai+KA_{i+K}Ai+K​. Can you sort the sequence this way?
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 1000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains N integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "yes" if the array can be sorted using the allowed swaps, otherwise "no".

sampleTestCases.explanation: With K=1 any adjacent swap is allowed, so the first array can be sorted. With K=2 positions 1 and 3 belong to one class and 2 and 4 to another; the required sorted order cannot be achieved.
Residue class 0 (indices 1,3,5) contains {3,4,5} while the sorted array would need {1,3,5} at those positions, so sorting is impossible.

solution.approach: Elements can only move inside positions that have the same index modulo K because a swap always connects i with i+K. Therefore each residue class (i mod K) forms an independent multiset. The whole array can be sorted iff for every residue class the multiset of original elements equals the multiset of elements that should appear at those positions in the fully sorted array. Algorithm: copy the array, sort the copy, create K buckets for original and sorted arrays based on index%K, sort each bucket and compare. If all buckets match, answer is "yes" else "no".
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (dominated by sorting the whole array).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the copy of the array and the buckets.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data=list(map(int,sys.stdin.read().split()))
    it=iter(data)
    T=next(it)
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=next(it); K=next(it)
        a=[next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        b=sorted(a)
        buckets_orig=[[] for _ in range(K)]
        buckets_sorted=[[] for _ in range(K)]
        for i,val in enumerate(a):
            buckets_orig[i%K].append(val)
        for i,val in enumerate(b):
            buckets_sorted[i%K].append(val)
        ok=True
        for r in range(K):
            if sorted(buckets_orig[r])!=sorted(buckets_sorted[r]):
                ok=False
                break
        out.append('yes' if ok else 'no')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
